title: Withdraw ₳15,750,000 for a MBO for the Cardano ecosystem: Intersect

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (enacted), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Why: Pinned anchor document is available for this action. Additional context: Treasury withdrawal actions require elevated scrutiny. Confidence context: score -0.20; confidence 72.3%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is above the hard-fork threshold; treated as a positive context signal.
top_fixes: Use stable, reproducible anchor links (commit-pinned when possible).
Add line-item budget with milestone-gated disbursement and objective completion checks.
Document downside risks, mitigations, and what gets cut first if delivery slips.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw ₳15,750,000 for a MBO for the Cardano ecosystem: Intersect

Deliverables: This treasury withdrawal funds A member-based organization for the Cardano ecosystem: Intersect which will provide the following services: Intersect exists to serve as a neutral, nonprofit coordination layer that bridges decentralized governance and decentr..., Our mission is not to direct the network, but to enable it., By stewarding core systems, supporting community-led processes, and facilitating safe, transparent delivery., Specifically, this proposal will fund Intersect to: - Steward core infrastructure - Execute the Administrator role - Support participation and coordination - Facilitate roadmap and budget processes - Expand ecosystem capacity Note: Intersect's original prop...

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal funds A member-based organization for the Cardano ecosystem: Intersect which will provide the following services: Intersect exists to serve as a neutral, nonprofit coordination layer that bridges decentralized governance and decentr...
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Our mission is not to direct the network, but to enable it.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): By stewarding core systems, supporting community-led processes, and facilitating safe, transparent delivery.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Specifically, this proposal will fund Intersect to: - Steward core infrastructure - Execute the Administrator role - Support participation and coordination - Facilitate roadmap and budget processes - Expand ecosystem capacity Note: Intersect's original prop...

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): This proposal aims to solve the following problem: As Cardano enters the age of Voltaire, governance is transitioning from a small number of founding organizations to a decentralized global community.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): While the introduction of a constitution and on-chain voting forms the structural backbone, these mechanisms alone are not enough to sustain a functional decentralized system.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds A member-based organization for the Cardano ecosystem: Intersect which will provide the following services: Intersect exists to serve as a neutral, nonprofit coordination layer that bridges decentralized governance and decentr..." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of TreasuryWithdrawals.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
